questions.question: What is the purpose of tsconfig.json?
options: A) To list all TypeScript files in a project
B) To configure the TypeScript compiler options and project settings
C) To define npm scripts
D) To store environment variables

questions.question: How do you generate a tsconfig.json file?
options: A) npm init tsconfig
B) tsc --init
C) npx create-tsconfig
D) typescript --config

questions.question: What does the 'target' compiler option specify?
options: A) The target operating system
B) The ECMAScript version the output JavaScript should conform to
C) The target browser
D) The TypeScript version to use

questions.question: If you set "target": "ES2020", what happens?
options: A) TypeScript only accepts ES2020 syntax
B) The compiled JavaScript uses ES2020 features (no downleveling for those features)
C) Only ES2020 browsers can run the code
D) TypeScript uses ES2020 type checking

questions.question: What does the 'module' compiler option control?
options: A) Which npm modules are available
B) The module system used in the generated JavaScript output (CommonJS, ESNext, etc.)
C) Whether modules can be imported
D) The module resolution algorithm only

questions.question: What does "strict": true enable?
options: A) Only strictNullChecks
B) A family of strict type-checking flags including strictNullChecks, noImplicitAny, and others
C) Strict JavaScript parsing mode
D) Strict file naming conventions

questions.question: Which flag does 'strict: true' NOT include?
options: A) strictNullChecks
B) noImplicitAny
C) noUnusedLocals
D) strictFunctionTypes

questions.question: What does 'strictNullChecks' do when enabled?
options: A) Prevents using null anywhere
B) Makes null and undefined their own types that must be explicitly handled
C) Automatically converts null to undefined
D) Removes null from all types

questions.question: What does 'noImplicitAny' do?
options: A) Bans the use of 'any' type entirely
B) Reports an error when TypeScript would infer the 'any' type for a parameter or variable
C) Converts all 'any' to 'unknown'
D) Makes 'any' behave like 'unknown'

questions.question: What does the 'outDir' option specify?
options: A) The directory where source files are located
B) The directory where compiled JavaScript files are placed
C) The output format for files
D) The directory for node_modules

questions.question: What does the 'rootDir' option specify?
options: A) The project root directory
B) The root folder of input source files, used to control the output directory structure
C) The npm root directory
D) The root of the file system

questions.question: What are declaration files (.d.ts)?
options: A) Compiled JavaScript files
B) Files containing only type information, no implementation
C) Configuration files for TypeScript
D) Test specification files

questions.question: What does "declaration": true do in tsconfig.json?
options: A) Declares the project as a TypeScript project
B) Generates .d.ts declaration files alongside the compiled JavaScript
C) Enables type declarations in code
D) Creates a declarations folder

questions.question: What does "sourceMap": true do?
options: A) Creates a map of all source files
B) Generates .map files that link compiled JS back to the original TS for debugging
C) Maps imports to file paths
D) Creates a visual diagram of the project

questions.question: Why are source maps useful?
options: A) They make code run faster
B) They allow debuggers to show TypeScript source instead of compiled JavaScript
C) They reduce file size
D) They enable hot module reloading

questions.question: What does the 'include' array in tsconfig.json do?
options: A) Lists npm packages to include
B) Specifies glob patterns for files to include in compilation
C) Includes external configuration files
D) Adds polyfills to the build

questions.question: What does the 'exclude' array in tsconfig.json do?
options: A) Excludes types from the type system
B) Specifies glob patterns for files to exclude from compilation
C) Removes packages from node_modules
D) Hides files from VS Code

questions.question: What is commonly excluded in tsconfig.json?
options: A) src/ directory
B) node_modules, dist, and test files (depending on config)
C) package.json
D) All .ts files

questions.question: What does "esModuleInterop": true do?
options: A) Enables ES module syntax in the output
B) Adds interoperability helpers for importing CommonJS modules with default import syntax
C) Converts all modules to ES modules
D) Disables CommonJS support

questions.question: What does "resolveJsonModule": true allow?
options: A) Parsing JSON at compile time
B) Importing .json files directly in TypeScript with type safety
C) Converting TypeScript to JSON
D) Using JSON as configuration

questions.question: What does "skipLibCheck": true do?
options: A) Skips checking your own library code
B) Skips type checking of declaration files (.d.ts), improving build speed
C) Skips installing library dependencies
D) Ignores type errors in your code

questions.question: What is the 'lib' compiler option used for?
options: A) Specifying external libraries to install
B) Specifying which built-in type declaration files (e.g., DOM, ES2020) to include
C) Setting the library output folder
D) Choosing the standard library implementation

questions.question: What does "moduleResolution": "node" mean?
options: A) Only Node.js modules are allowed
B) TypeScript uses Node.js-style module resolution (checking node_modules, index files, etc.)
C) Modules resolve to node binaries
D) Enables Node.js built-in modules only

questions.question: What does "noEmit": true do?
options: A) Prevents TypeScript from emitting errors
B) Runs type checking only without generating output files
C) Disables console output
D) Prevents importing external modules

questions.question: What does the 'extends' field in tsconfig.json do?
options: A) Extends the TypeScript language with new features
B) Inherits compiler options from another tsconfig file
C) Extends the project's file system scope
D) Adds additional type definitions
